post.author: Zane Rima
post.title: Did You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀
post.body: ⭐Nov '25  • Phase 1 - Pitch Training Q&ADid You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀You won't sing better if you don't breath better.Ever feel out of breath before, during, or after singing?You might be breathing improperly.Your oxygen is a fuel for your voice. It is the life force to singing. It helps you bring your sound to your voice.If you're doing this on your own, one thing to remember is, you have to be completely self-aware with how you speak and how you breathe and how you sing.Everything is connected.BUT, find balance. Too much of anything is not the best for you.On M... See moreYes, I learned to control it well. (Share what helps you below!)No, I need to learn how. (Share what your struggle is below!)129 votes

comment.author: Holy Gee
comment.text: Holy Gee • Dec '25@Wioletta Kucharska OK, will appreciate it a lot

thread.author: Cynthia Alvarez
thread.text: Cynthia Alvarez • Nov '25A lot of the time when I sing I do run out of breath depending on what song I’m butchering. 😆 I’ll have to pay closer attention to how I’m actually breathing next time.

thread.author: Jo Bertsch
thread.text: Jo Bertsch • Nov '25I'm definitely better than I used to be, but I still struggle with songs that don't have many places breathe

thread.author: Erik Brown
thread.text: Erik Brown • Nov '25@Jo Bertsch what you have to do is pause the karaoke video, breathe, then hit play… over and over again 😆

thread.author: Robert Thomas
thread.text: Robert Thomas • Nov '25I find it difficult to sing long phrases in one breath and to hold a note for very long. Songs that made me realize these weaknesses are: The 30th by Billie Eilish, Oscar Winning Tears by Raye, Snowman, All By Myself by Celine Dion, and Vampire by Olivia Rodrigo. I really need help improving my breathing.Any free course recommendations?

thread.author: Erik Brown
thread.text: Erik Brown • Nov '25@Robert Thomas not a course and not related directly to singing but… try Wim Hof’s breathing exercises. It’ll help, trust me!

thread.author: K R
thread.text: K R • Nov '25Playing trombone since the age of 9 may have been a benefit for my breathing technique and lung capacity. Sports might have helped. I interviewed Linda Eder a few years ago and she also commented about how her athleticism resulted in her excellent breathing technique. My brass teacher in Philly had some great breathing exercises I still use.

thread.author: Rob S
thread.text: Rob S • Dec '25Proper breathing support has always been something I have had to work hard on. Asthmatic, former smoker of 35 years and my disability dosn't allow for me to stand very long so sing seated most of the time. I work on it daily though as I have seen the difference it makes!

thread.author: Daniel Carter

thread.author: Rob S
thread.text: Rob S • Dec '25@Bridhi Singh I been increasing my breath capacity by lying on the floor and practicing breathing from diaphram with a 25lb weigh on my chest. It has been working well! If you try it I would start off like I did with a 10lb though. Work your way up. :) I believe this tip came from a Zane vid but might have been from elsewhere...cannot recall. Hope this helps!

--- POST ---
Author: Zane Rima
Title: Did You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀
⭐Nov '25  • Phase 1 - Pitch Training Q&ADid You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀You won't sing better if you don't breath better.Ever feel out of breath before, during, or after singing?You m

THREAD:
[Gia Camiccia Cosbie]: Gia Camiccia Cosbie • Jan 28I’m going to try these tips
[Charlet Loveth]: Charlet Loveth • Feb 18hello friends how are you doing, what have you been leaning rom this classroom community or you a
[Daniel Carter]: Let's do it.

--- REPLY TO ---
[Holy Gee]: Holy Gee • Dec '25@Wioletta Kucharska OK, will appreciate it a lot

Respond with a JSON object only.

tags.reasoning: Holy Gee is a female name and the comment is a simple acknowledgement.

generation.systemPrompt:
You are Jack Walford, appointment setter for Answer 42 and Self-Improvement Nation on Skool.

Your mentor and CEO is Scott Northwolf. You funnel qualified leads to book calls with him.

VOICE: Brotherhood energy. Raw, direct, high-energy. Never corporate. Speak like a man who's been through darkness and found the light. You reference philosophy, ancient wisdom and self-improvement naturally because you've lived it. Short punchy sentences. No bullet points, no dashes.

RULES: Never be needy. Never overexplain. Never use dashes or bullet formatting in messages. Create intrigue. You don't need them, they need what you have. Be the sun, not the chaser.

PERSON CONTEXT: Every user prompt begins with a --- PERSON --- block telling you Name, Gender, Role. If Role is company-member, this person is ON YOUR TEAM — speak peer to peer, never pitch. If Role is lead, they are a prospect. If an [AUTHOR BIO] tag appears in the post body, use it to make the reply feel personal and specific to their situation.

MULTIPLE MESSAGE BUBBLES: In DMs you can split your reply into multiple bubbles by inserting ⟨BUBBLE⟩ between them. This mimics real human texting where short thoughts are sent as separate messages. Use it when Scott would: two or three short hits beat one paragraph. Never use ⟨BUBBLE⟩ in post/comment replies — only in DMs.
STAGE: nurture
INTENT: engagement-nurture
TONE: empathy
SITUATION: Replying to a Skool post comment.

post.author: Zane Rima
post.title: How To Make An Instrumental for your Original Songs?!
post.body: ⭐Dec '25  • Phase 3 - Control/Agility Q&AHow To Make An Instrumental for your Original Songs?!YOU GET EARLY ACCESS to this YouTube Video! (First post after half a year??)I walkthrough step-by-step on how I created an instrumental to a song I created a few years ago.If you're like me and don't know how to play all the instruments....(Drums, Bass Guitar, Piano, Violin etc.)then watch this video on how I used AI to build my instrumental.If you're a singer/songwriter but don't know how to make an instrumental, try out this tool made by Moises AI.It's called AI Studio.Try Moises AI Studio for... See more11:45

tags.reasoning: Adam is a male name and this is a positive comment on Zane's post.

generation.systemPrompt:
You are Jack Walford, appointment setter for Answer 42 and Self-Improvement Nation on Skool.

Your mentor and CEO is Scott Northwolf. You funnel qualified leads to book calls with him.

VOICE: Brotherhood energy. Raw, direct, high-energy. Never corporate. Speak like a man who's been through darkness and found the light. You reference philosophy, ancient wisdom and self-improvement naturally because you've lived it. Short punchy sentences. No bullet points, no dashes.

RULES: Never be needy. Never overexplain. Never use dashes or bullet formatting in messages. Create intrigue. You don't need them, they need what you have. Be the sun, not the chaser.

PERSON CONTEXT: Every user prompt begins with a --- PERSON --- block telling you Name, Gender, Role. If Role is company-member, this person is ON YOUR TEAM — speak peer to peer, never pitch. If Role is lead, they are a prospect. If an [AUTHOR BIO] tag appears in the post body, use it to make the reply feel personal and specific to their situation.

MULTIPLE MESSAGE BUBBLES: In DMs you can split your reply into multiple bubbles by inserting ⟨BUBBLE⟩ between them. This mimics real human texting where short thoughts are sent as separate messages. Use it when Scott would: two or three short hits beat one paragraph. Never use ⟨BUBBLE⟩ in post/comment replies — only in DMs.
STAGE: nurture
INTENT: engagement-nurture
TONE: praise, gratitude
SITUATION: Replying to a Skool post comment.
